code: entity_recall <- function(ms, tiers = c("clean", "slogan", "variant", "mover", "phonetic")) {   ent   <- ms |> select(id, entity)   truth <- workshop_listings |>     filter(!is.na(actual_link)) |>     select(listing_id, actual_link, gen_tier)    truth |>     left_join(ent, by = c("listing_id" = "id")) |>     left_join(ent, by = c("actual_link" = "id"), suffix = c("_listing", "_register")) |>     mutate(linked = !is.na(entity_listing) & entity_listing == entity_register) |>     filter(gen_tier %in% tiers) |>     group_by(gen_tier) |>     summarise(recovered = sum(linked), total = n(), .groups = "drop") |>     arrange(match(gen_tier, tiers)) }

title: joinery — Developer / Coding-Agent Guide
text: Preprocessing — prepare_search_data() interprets Step objects, produces long-form tokens, attaches block variables. Rarity Computation — measures token informativeness per column/block using inverse frequency, TF-IDF, metrics. Token Overlap Join — self-join (duplicates) cross-join (candidates) (column, token, block_by) find record pairs. Scoring (rIP) — relative identification potential: rIP = rarity / sum(rarity) per record, score = sum(rIP * weight). Thresholding — keep pairs score >= threshold (argument strategy default). Residual Generation — extract unmatched records via extract_unmatched() multi-pass workflows. Staged entity resolution — ordered strategy list run successive passes, accumulating links resolving connected components via resolve_entities(). Two faces (§34): multi_stage_dedup() (within one table, one final CC, residual carry-forward keeps group’s representative bridge) multi_stage_search() (cross-source directed search → entity grouping + directed ledger, generic source_by, collapse="rep" collapse--continue bridging). wrap shared engine R/internal_staging.R.

text: Five diagnostic verbs, organised around four user questions (Q1 --work, Q2 --work, Q3 --pair, Q4 --look) plus multi-stage: audit_strategy() → Strategy_Audit (token strategies) Embedding_Audit (embedding strategies) — dispatches strategy class summarise_matches() → Match_Overview (unified across dedup/candidates via match_type slot) explain_match() → Match_Explanation (per-token attribution Search_Strategy; pair+score Embedding_Strategy) sample_matches() → Match_Sample (modes: high, low, borderline, ambiguous, top_gap, random; also stratify_by expand_to_block stratified labelling-set construction) compare_stages() → Stage_Comparison (multi-stage diagnostics) rarity_distribution() → Rarity_Distribution (pre-match, read-side; v0.8 Stage 04). Scoring-free prepare_search_data() + compute_rarity() — overlap join — reporting per-(column[, block]) df/rarity distribution top-df offender list (fan-drivers), plus suggested_min_rarity per column. Use set min_rarity / max_token_df levers real token distribution. Lives R/diagnostic_rarity.R; cheap seed plan_strategy() subsumes. plan_strategy(base, strategy, target = NULL, block_candidates, base_id, target_id) → Strategy_Plan (pre-match, pre-strategy; v0.8 Stage 08). verb helps find strategy rather grade one — upstream distinct audit_strategy() (multi-block, scoring-free, dispatches c("base", "strategy"); strategy supplies preparer pipeline, block_by ignored — block_candidates thing chosen). Deliberately scoring-free: overlap join, .score_*; every probe O(rows)/O(blocks) arithmetic. Four reads: (1) blocking-resolution frontier — per candidate: #blocks, size distribution, Σ(na·nb) brute-pair count (cost axis, arithmetic), share exact-token-set twins stay co-blocked (recall axis), using A2’s faithful fingerprint (.exact_fp_wide_dt via exact proxy, never re-rolled); (2) exact-set persister rate (A2 yield — exact_strategy() front absorb enough stage?); (3) residual structure (matchable / one-sided / per-column partial-recoverable); (4) per-column discriminativeness — .rarity_distribution_core + min_rarity → intermediate-overlap-row cost curve (pure df-histogram math; matches independent overlap-row count exactly) + §25 empty-column score-ceiling read (1 − normalized weight(col)) + opt-§22 containment share (containment = TRUE — one read bounded structural join; NA default scoring-free guarantee holds). DuckDB samples SELECT * delegates data.table (pairs touch connection — scoring-free guard). Lives R/plan_strategy.R (class colocated, like diagnostic_rarity.R); default plot() frontier_plot(). Part-spine’s final stage. Recommendations live R/diagnostics_recommendations.R (signal → threshold → message), surfaced via inline cli warnings print() recommendations(x) accessor. plan_strategy() adds four: blocking_knee (coarser candidate near-lossless twins materially cheaper), empty_column_ceiling (§25), consider_containment (§22, opt-), est_comparisons_too_high (cheapest candidate still dense). Plotting first-class via tinyplot (hard Imports dependency). Diagnostic verbs return data . plot separately named function (plot(x, type=...)); pipe-composable: summarise_matches(m) |> score_histogram(). Default plot() methods per class call -useful single view. explain_match round-trip contract (sum(per_column_contrib$contribution) × feedback_factor == score, exact 1e-10 feedback) mandatory backends — property test prevents scoring drift.

what: How joinery differs from classical record linkage
title: Heuristic Index-Based Record Linkage in R
text: record linkage tools rely edit distances probabilistic pairwise comparison blocking. works well clean data short strings, often breaks messy administrative data rare tokens matter character-level similarity. joinery follows different paradigm inspired Doherr’s SearchEngine: Matching framed search candidate retrieval, exhaustive pairwise comparison. Records decomposed tokens weighted informativeness (rarity). Similarity comes overlap informative tokens, string distance. Candidate generation scoring operation. Matching directional; one table defines token frequencies, provides search queries. approach works especially well : Names long, noisy, inconsistently formatted. Rare identifiers dominate common boilerplate terms. labeled training data available. Matching proceeds multiple, increasingly tolerant stages. also runs data fit memory. strategy prototype -memory table runs unchanged DuckDB database, large joins --core, disk, piece time. Little else R links resolves entities way: practice turned 51-million-row business directory, job classically call cluster, overnight run laptop. joinery drop-replacement probabilistic linkage engines. complementary tool transparent, strategy-driven matching robustness explainability matter calibrated match probabilities. place among alternatives: reach fuzzyjoin zoomerjoin need string- edit-distance joins short, mostly clean fields; reach fastLink reclin2 need Fellegi-Sunter model calibrated m/u match probabilities. Reach joinery records long messy, rare tokens carry signal, want see exactly pair matched. probabilistic scoring strategy roadmap, boundary expected narrow time.

text: example uses package’s built-sample data. Getting started vignette walks path step step scores result known answer key (target_example$actual_link holds true link copied record).
code: library(joinery)  # Load example data (shipped as tibbles; joinery also takes data.frames / data.table / DuckDB) data("base_example") data("target_example")  # Define a simple first-pass strategy strat <- search_strategy(   Nachname   ~ normalize_text() + word_tokens(min_nchar = 3),   Vorname    ~ normalize_text() + word_tokens(min_nchar = 3),   Strasse    ~ normalize_street(lang = "de") + word_tokens(min_nchar = 3),   Hausnummer ~ numeric_tokens,   Ort        ~ normalize_text(),   block_by   = "Kreis",   threshold  = 0.8 )  # Inspect the tokenization inspect_tokens(base_example, "id_base", strat, Vorname)  # Detect and collapse duplicates within the base data duplicates    <- detect_duplicates(base_example, id = "id_base", strategy = strat) base_dedupped <- deduplicate_table(base_example, duplicates, id = "id_base")  # Cross-table candidate matches matches <- search_candidates(   base_dedupped,   target_example,   base_id   = "id_base",   target_id = "id_target",   strategy  = strat )  # Check the result, then read the residuals summarise_matches(matches, threshold = 0.8) extract_unmatched(base_dedupped, "id_base", matches) extract_unmatched(target_example, "id_target", matches)

what: Block on a Column's Rare Tokens (region-free blocking) — block_on_tokens
title: Block on a Column's Rare Tokens (region-free blocking) — block_on_tokens
text: Build token-blocking key use inside strategy's block_by. plain column name blocks two records share literal value, block_on_tokens() blocks share designated column's (rare) tokens. region-free: record drifts across region boundary - firm moves new postcode, say - still co-blocks earlier self distinctive name token, becomes candidate literal block never compare . Hand block_by place (mixed ) column name:   max_df min_rarity select tokens eligible block keys, using global (corpus-wide) document frequency: token appearing max_df records, whose global rarity falls min_rarity, dropped key. "block distinctive words, common ones" lives - franchise name ("ALDI") globally common, fails cap, never becomes block key, distinctive brand survives. record surviving block key unreachable via token-blocking stage (contributes token-block rows). Token-blocking densest operation package: every pair sharing surviving key materialised. safe behind real max_df (min_rarity) plus always-fan-guard. Passing neither cap loud warning, error, almost always want one.
code: # fully region-free - share a rare name token, regardless of place search_strategy(name ~ normalize_text + word_tokens(min_nchar = 3),                 block_by = block_on_tokens("name", max_df = 50))  # region-bounded - share a rare name token AND sit in the same plz2 search_strategy(name ~ normalize_text + word_tokens(min_nchar = 3),                 block_by = list(block_on_tokens("name", max_df = 50), "plz2"))

title: Block on a Column's Rare Tokens (region-free blocking) — block_on_tokens

title: Block on a Column's Rare Tokens (region-free blocking) — block_on_tokens
text: column column whose tokens become block keys (example "name"). max_df Numeric scalar. Global document-frequency cap: tokens appearing max_df records corpus-wide dropped block keys. Default Inf (cap - see density warning ). min_rarity Numeric scalar. Global rarity floor: tokens whose corpus-wide rarity falls dropped block keys. Default 0. preparer Optional preprocessing pipeline blocking column, given one-sided two-sided formula like column ~ steps pass search_strategy() (example ~ normalize_text + word_tokens(min_nchar = 4)). Default NULL reuses column's scored preparer column also scored column, else falls back normalize_text + word_tokens(min_nchar = min_nchar). min_nchar Integer scalar. Minimum token length default preparer. Default 3L.

The max_df cap keeps # common words ("joinery") from becoming block keys. strat <- search_strategy(   workshop ~ normalize_text() + word_tokens(min_nchar = 3),   block_by     = list(block_on_tokens("workshop", max_df = 50, min_nchar = 4),                       "trade"),   rarity_scope = "global",   threshold    = 0.6 ) strat #> <joinery::Search_Strategy> #>  #> columns #> workshop: normalize_text() -> word_tokens(min_nchar = 3) #>  #> blocking: block_on_tokens(workshop, max_df=50), trade #> weights: none #> rarity: inverse_freq (global, min=0) #> fan-out guard: cap at 50,000,000 #> smoothing: none #> threshold: 0.6 #> max_candidates: none #> feedback_strength: none

title: Create a Batch Plan for DuckDB Table Processing — duckdb_batch_plan

title: Create a Batch Plan for DuckDB Table Processing — duckdb_batch_plan
text: db_tbl DuckDB table reference (result dplyr::tbl(con, "table_name")) id Character. Column name(s) use record identifier(s). used batching validated exist table. target_batch_size Positive integer. Target number rows per batch. Default: 1e6 (1 million rows). min_batch_size Positive integer. Minimum table size trigger batching. total rows < min_batch_size, returns single batch. Default: 1e5 (100k rows). chunk_strategy Character. One "even", "block_first", "block_consolidated". Default: "block_consolidated". block_by Optional character vector. Column name(s) use semantic blocking. specified, batches respect block boundaries. Supports multiple columns (e.g., c("region", "year")). atomic_blocks Logical. FALSE (default) planner may sub-split block larger target_batch_size row-number windows - correct preprocess batching, token generation per-row independent. TRUE (scoring path) block treated indivisible: small blocks consolidated budget large block kept whole single chunk (flagged oversized = TRUE), never sub-split - match pair forms within block, splitting one silently drop cross-pairs. Requires block_by; rejects chunk_strategy = "even".

title: Create a Batch Plan for DuckDB Table Processing — duckdb_batch_plan
text: data.table columns: batch_id: integer, sequential batch identifier (1, 2, 3, ...) row_count: integer, number rows batch row_start: integer (NA), window start row-number-based batches; NA block-based row_end: integer (NA), window end row-number-based batches; NA block-based Additional columns (block_by specified): one per blocking variable, containing block values

title: Create a Batch Plan for DuckDB Table Processing — duckdb_batch_plan
text: function supports three chunking strategies: "even": Simple row-number chunking, ignores blocks "block_first": batch = one block (sub-chunks block > target_batch_size) "block_consolidated": Consolidates small blocks minimize batch count (default) Small tables: total rows < min_batch_size, returns single batch regardless strategy. blocking, still respects blocks. Row-number windows: unblocked large-block sub-chunking, row_start row_end define window boundaries (1-based, inclusive). block-based batches (small blocks), NA. Consolidation: "block_consolidated" (default) combines multiple small blocks single batches target_batch_size reduce overhead. batch may contain zero, one, multiple blocks (depending sizes consolidation). Row ordering: ensure row_start row_end windows consecutive can reliably sliced DB, function sorts id column computing row numbers. ensures reproducible, deterministic batch boundaries.

title: Create a Batch Plan for DuckDB Table Processing — duckdb_batch_plan
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ con <- DBI::dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), ":memory:") DBI::dbWriteTable(con, "data", data.frame(id = 1:1e6, region = sample(LETTERS, 1e6, TRUE))) tbl_ref <- dplyr::tbl(con, "data")  # Unblocked, even row-number chunking plan1 <- duckdb_batch_plan(   tbl_ref, id = "id",   target_batch_size = 1e5, chunk_strategy = "even" )  # Blocked, consolidated strategy (default, respects regions) plan2 <- duckdb_batch_plan(   tbl_ref, id = "id",   target_batch_size = 1e5, block_by = "region" ) } # }

title: DuckDB Execution Control — duckdb_control
text: Build Duckdb_Control bundling DuckDB backend's execution knobs, pass control = prepare_search_data(), detect_duplicates(), search_candidates() DuckDB tables. controls match runs (memory, batching, chunking, failure isolation), never matches - matching semantics stay search_strategy(). Two execution stages, two atomicity rules: Preprocess batching (tokenization) per-row, governed target_batch_size / min_batch_size / chunk_strategy. row split safe. Scoring chunking (overlap join) block-atomic - pair forms within block, block can never split. chunk_by packs whole blocks target_batch_size; on_error isolates pathological block rest run. Chunking DuckDB (--core) concern; -memory data.table backend ignores .

title: DuckDB Execution Control — duckdb_control
code: duckdb_control(   target_batch_size = NULL,   min_batch_size = NULL,   chunk_strategy = c("block_consolidated", "block_first", "even"),   chunk_by = NULL,   on_error = c("skip", "retry", "stop"),   progress = NULL )

title: DuckDB Execution Control — duckdb_control
text: target_batch_size NULL (auto-tune RAM / row size) positive number rows per batch / scoring chunk. min_batch_size NULL (auto-tune) positive number - minimum table size preprocess batching engages. chunk_strategy Preprocess chunking strategy: "block_consolidated" (default), "block_first", "even". chunk_by Scoring chunk key. NULL (default) auto-derives coarse key input large leaves small inputs monolithic; FALSE forces monolithic path; character vector names explicit key, must subset strategy's block_by (else cross-chunk pairs silently dropped). on_error Per-scoring-chunk failure policy: "skip" (default - record continue), "retry" (re-run conservative pragmas, skip), "stop" (re-raise). progress NULL (auto), TRUE, FALSE force suppress progress output.

code: # The control object just bundles execution knobs; it carries no data. ctrl <- duckdb_control(target_batch_size = 5e5, on_error = "skip") ctrl #> <joinery::Duckdb_Control> #>  #> target_batch_size: 5e+05 #> min_batch_size: auto #> chunk_strategy: block_consolidated #> chunk_by: auto #> on_error: skip #> progress: auto  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Pass it to a verb running on a DuckDB table. library(duckdb) library(DBI) library(dplyr) con <- dbConnect(duckdb::duckdb(), ":memory:") dbWriteTable(con, "reg", as.data.frame(workshop_register)) strat <- search_strategy(   workshop ~ normalize_text() + word_tokens(min_nchar = 3),   block_by  = c("postcode_area", "trade"),   threshold = 0.7 ) detect_duplicates(tbl(con, "reg"), "reg_no", strat, control = ctrl) dbDisconnect(con, shutdown = TRUE) } # }

what: Define an Exact Matching Strategy — exact_strategy
title: Define an Exact Matching Strategy — exact_strategy
text: Creates Exact_Strategy exact, score-1.0 token-set matching. Hand verbs hand search_strategy(): detect_duplicates() group identical records within table, search_candidates() match across tables. return usual result score == 1.0. Two records link every column's token set equal within block. fuzzy score exactly 1.0, reached without scoring threshold, robust empty columns: two records identical names streets blank link, weighted threshold silently reject . (blank column drags weighted score threshold, since weight stays denominator; exact matching ceiling.) Use cheap first stage staged workflow (exact first, fuzzy whatever left): leftover records come extract_unmatched(), multi_stage_dedup() / multi_stage_search() thread pass list(exact_strategy(...), search_strategy(...)).

title: Define an Exact Matching Strategy — exact_strategy
code: exact_strategy(   ...,   block_by = NULL,   rarity = "inverse_freq",   containment = c("off", "forward", "bidirectional"),   min_base_rarity = 0,   min_containment_tokens = 1 )

title: Define an Exact Matching Strategy — exact_strategy
text: ... Two-sided formulas column ~ step1 + step2, identical form search_strategy(). block_by Optional character vector blocking columns. rarity Character scalar rarity metric, used min_base_rarity containment guard measure much identifying weight base record's tokens carry. One "inverse_freq" (default), "smoothed_inverse_freq", "tfidf", "bm25"; see search_strategy() formula . Plain equality forward containment without min_base_rarity floor never consult , default almost always fine. containment One "" (set-equality, default), "forward" (link base record's tokens subset target's), "bidirectional" (either direction). Whether helps depends data; -links noisy corpora, never default. min_base_rarity Numeric containment guard: drop links whose base record carries summed rarity mass floor. Default 0. min_containment_tokens Numeric containment guard, default 1 (restriction). proper containment link (one record's tokens strict subset 's) requires contained record hold least many tokens; set-equality always links. Raise 2 stop single generic token - bare category hub name (e.g. shopping-centre name) - subset every richer "Store + Centre" name transitively chaining unrelated records one entity. Ignored containment = "".

Method-specific arguments. strategy methods accept: base (base table used input matching), id (character scalar naming ID column base), target (optional target table cross-table candidate matches), target_id (ID column target, defaults id), include_string_sim (logical; TRUE (default) emits sim_sf_<col> / sim_fs_<col> per column via stringdist::stringsim() - requires stringdist suggested package), method (stringdist method applied every column, default "jw". scalar honoured today; argument shape also reserves named character vector per-column methods, additive path per-column comparators future probabilistic strategy use), include_block_stats (logical; whether compute cnt / icnt / ipos). Search_Strategy method additionally accepts top_n (named integer / list controlling per-column top-N counts m_/f_/s_ columns; use default entry fallback; set column 0 suppress set). title: Normalize street names across languages — normalize_street
text: Returns text, sits normalize_text() pipeline, ahead token generator: street ~ normalize_street(lang = "de") + word_tokens(). Exact matches (e.g., "st", "rd.", "via") always replaced. Suffix matches (e.g., German "strasse" endings Dutch "straat") applied lang explicitly specified, prevents unsafe substitutions rewriting ending "LINCOLN LANE". Normalization steps include: Unicode -> Latin transliteration ASCII folding (stri_trans_general) Conversion uppercase Removal non-alphanumeric characters Tokenization spaces per-token replacement Exact variants replaced verbatim canonical form. Suffix variants replaced : lang specified, token ends known variant suffix language.

title: Normalize street names across languages — normalize_street
code: normalize_street("Muellerstrasse", lang = "de") #> [1] "MUELLERSTRASSE" # "MUELLERSTRASSE"  normalize_street("123 Main St.") #> [1] "123 MAIN STREET" # "123 MAIN STREET"  normalize_street("Calle Mayor 3", lang = "es") #> [1] "CALLE MAYOR 3" # "CALLE MAYOR 3"  normalize_street("Hauptstr. 123A", lang = "de", drop_house_numbers = TRUE) #> [1] "HAUPTSTRASSE" # "HAUPTSTRASSE"  normalize_street("An der Alster 5", lang = "de",                  drop_house_numbers = TRUE, drop_stopwords = TRUE) #> [1] "ALSTER" # "ALSTER"

title: Plan a Search Strategy from Raw Inputs — plan_strategy

title: Plan a Search Strategy from Raw Inputs — plan_strategy
text: base data.frame / tibble / data.table (backend table). strategy Search_Strategy supplying tokenization plan . target Optional second table. NULL (default) plans dedup; non-NULL plans cross-table search. block_candidates Named list candidate block_by specs compare (e.g. list(plz2 = "plz2", plz5_wz = c("plz5", "wz08_3"))). base_id Character scalar naming id column base (required). target_id Character scalar naming id column target (defaults base_id). n_offenders Number top-df "offender" tokens (fan-drivers) report per column. Defaults 20. min_rarity_grid Optional numeric vector min_rarity cut points cost curve. NULL (default) picks grid rarity distribution. containment Logical. TRUE, adds per-column containment share, one read performs bounded structural join. Defaults FALSE, keeps plan_strategy() scoring-free. ... Backend-specific arguments, sample_n (DuckDB).

what: Group Matched Pairs into Entities — resolve_entities
title: Group Matched Pairs into Entities — resolve_entities
text: Take list matched record pairs (edge list) turn entities: records link directly chain links grouped together, group gets entity number, one record group marked representative. grouping step detect_duplicates() performs internally, exposed can resolve pair list entities, example output search_candidates() set links assembled .

title: Group Matched Pairs into Entities — resolve_entities
code: resolve_entities(   edges,   id_a,   id_b,   score = NULL,   vertices = NULL,   rep_by = NULL,   block_by = NULL,   ... )

title: Group Matched Pairs into Entities — resolve_entities
text: edges backend table record-pair edges (one row per edge). id_a, id_b Character scalars naming two endpoint columns edges. score Optional character scalar naming per-edge score column edges. supplied, within-entity rank ordered descending best score (maximum vertex's incident edges) best score returned extra score column. NULL, ranking falls back rep_by/id rule. vertices Optional. vertex ids include, ids absent every edge come back singleton entity (rank 1, rep = self). Either atomic vector ids, table id column (plus rep_by column). NULL, ids appearing edges returned. rep_by Optional character scalar naming priority column (vertices table) used pick canonical representative: member smallest rep_by wins, ties broken smallest id. block_by Optional character vector columns edges used run connected components per block (DuckDB backend). ... Additional arguments passed backend-specific methods.

title: Group Matched Pairs into Entities — resolve_entities
text: One row per resolved vertex: id vertex id. entity Integer entity (connected-component) label. rep canonical representative id entity. rank Rank within entity; rank 1 representative. score Best incident-edge score per vertex (score supplied).

title: Group Matched Pairs into Entities — resolve_entities
text: result depend order rows edges: pairs always produce entity, rep, rank. Entity numbers assigned smallest member id group. representative (rank-1 member) chosen highest best score score column given, smallest rep_by given, smallest id.

title: Group Matched Pairs into Entities — resolve_entities

title: Multilingual Street-Type Normalization Dictionary — street_types
text: Unlike simple suffix lists, dictionary encodes language-specific normalization rules. variant marked either: "exact" - variant match token appears exactly, e.g. "st." -> "STREET" (English), "pl" -> "PLAZA" (Spanish) "suffix" - variant may safely match token ending sequence, e.g. "gatan" -> "GATA" (Swedish), "strasse" -> "STRASSE" (German) separating exact vs. suffix behaviour tagging entry ISO language code, joinery can normalize addresses without incorrect transformations (e.g. preventing "LINCOLN" -> "LANE", "VICTOR" -> "RUE"). structure enables high-precision multilingual address cleaning.

title: Multilingual Street-Type Normalization Dictionary — street_types
text: dictionary currently includes major street-type systems : German - Straße, Gasse, Weg, Platz, Allee, etc. English - Street, Road, Avenue, Boulevard, Lane, etc. French - Rue, Avenue, Boulevard, Impasse, Quai, Chemin, etc. Spanish - Calle, Avenida, Paseo, Plaza, Camino, etc. Italian - Via, Piazza, Corso, Viale, etc. Portuguese - Rua, Avenida, Praça, Alameda, Travessa, etc. Polish - Ulica, Aleja, Plac, Osiedle, etc. Dutch - Straat, Laan, Weg, Plein, etc. Turkish - Sokak, Cadde, Bulvar, Meydan, etc. Swedish - Gata, Gatan, Vägen, Torg, etc. Danish/Norwegian - Gade, Vej, Plads, etc. Greek (transliterated) - Odos, Leoforos, Plateia Russian (transliterated) - Ulitsa, Prospekt, Pereulok, etc. Additional languages street-type systems can incorporated needed.

text: Run strategies order, carry residuals forward, resolve entities end. multi_stage_dedup() multi_stage_search(): run ordered list strategies successive passes. multi_stage_dedup() finds duplicates within one table; multi_stage_search() links records across tables, across years one pooled table self = TRUE. take mix exact, fuzzy, embedding strategies; multi_stage_search() supports collapse--continue slowly drifting name links one step time. Renames multi_stage_match(). exact_strategy(): identical-token-set matching strategy, cheap first pass. Runs detect_duplicates() search_candidates() like strategy. Optional containment matches subset rather exact set, per-column min_containment_tokens floor. resolve_entities(): group edge list entities (connected components) pick representative per group. materialize_records(): fetch original rows set ids, complement extract_unmatched(). plan_strategy(): compare blocking keys matching. Reports candidate’s block sizes, comparison cost, many true twins stay co-blocked, without computing scores. rarity_distribution(): report column’s token frequency rarity matching, suggested min_rarity. find_stopwords(): list column’s high-frequency, low-information tokens filter_stopwords(). duckdb_control(): one object DuckDB execution tuning (batch size, scoring chunk key, per-chunk failure policy, progress), passed control =. title: joinery 0.8.0
text: Internal consolidation calibration work, plus fixes surfaced full-scale Yellow-Pages panel build. Output schemas unchanged. Token-set scoring: token repeated within one record longer inflates pair’s score; scores stay within [0, sum(weights)]. DuckDB scale: connected components run per block instead one global recursion exhausted memory corpus scale; empty dedup results carry full schema; filtered lazy inputs (tbl |> filter(...)) accepted everywhere. summarise_matches(entity_cols =): count duplicate groups whose listed columns single-valued, separating real stopword clusters cardinality artefacts. Consistent errors: unified cli::cli_abort() rlang argument checks across exported verbs. File layout: R/ reorganised eight-prefix naming scheme (see CLAUDE.md).

text: optional post-match filter learns drop false positives small labelled sample. verb works token embedding strategies. match_features(): build one-row-per-pair feature table match result, token-overlap counts, auxiliary-side informativeness (aIP, Doherr 2023), string similarities. fit_filter() / apply_filter(): fit logistic false-positive filter apply , choosing threshold Youden’s J unless set one. calibrate_matches(): one verb composing features, fit, apply. calibrate(): evaluate fitted filter labelled set; returns reliability, Brier score, log-loss, confusion matrix, threshold sweep. Labelling helpers: sample_matches() stratification, plus export_for_labelling() / import_labels() CSV round-trip. title: joinery 0.6.0
text: Verbs answer four questions strategy results: work, work, pair, look. audit_strategy(): grade strategy matching. summarise_matches(): overview dedup candidate result, unified across backends. explain_match(): per-token attribution single pair’s score. sample_matches(): draw pairs mode (high, low, borderline, ambiguous, top-gap, random). compare_stages(): per-stage coverage multi-stage workflows. Diagnostic plots: family pipe-composable tinyplot functions, one per view. Recommendations: strategies results surface inline advice signal-driven catalog, also available via recommendations().

text: Optional semantic matching complements rather replaces token core. Use embeddings fields word-overlap fails (paraphrases, multilingual variants, fuzzy free-text descriptions) combine token strategies via multi_stage_match().

title: joinery 0.5.0
text: embedding_strategy(): declarative strategy embedding-based linkage, mirroring ergonomics search_strategy(). Specify one embedding columns, optional block_by, optional threshold, optional weights vector across embedding columns. Cosine-similarity scoring record-level embedding vectors, optional pre-normalization cosine reduces fast inner product scoring time. Strategies expose normalize flag users want keep raw magnitudes. Drop-compatibility existing verbs: detect_duplicates(), search_candidates(), extract_unmatched() accept Embedding_Strategy return standard joinery output schemas (duplicate_group / match_id, score, rank, original columns). Multi-stage token + embedding workflows: multi_stage_match() accepts sequence mixed Search_Strategy Embedding_Strategy objects, threading residuals stages stopping early either side exhausted. Useful pattern: cheap token stage first, embedding stage residual. block_by support embeddings cosine search runs within blocks (e.g. country, year bucket) instead across whole table. Backend parity: full implementation data.table, DuckDB, tibble / data.frame, call signatures across backends. DuckDB scales embedding search large tables via existing batch infrastructure. Embedding generation via tidyllm (optional Suggests dependency): provider-agnostic helpers Ollama, OpenAI, tidyllm-supported backends, users can move raw text matchable embedding column without leaving R. Embedding-aware diagnostics groundwork: strategy-class dispatch place Phase 0.6 diagnostics can specialise embedding strategies without API churn.
